final_prompt:
Create low-level requirements for the following high-level requirement.

Return JSON only, with no Markdown or explanatory prose, using exactly this schema:
{"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"service acronym","requirement":"requirement text"}]}
High-level requirement:
{HLR}
Prompt refinement guidance:
- Cover every distinct behavior, condition, and constraint expressed by the HLR.
- Do not add capabilities, technologies, constraints, or subsystem behavior not justified by the HLR.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QC service shall monitor incoming data streams in real time to detect when new data become available for quality checking.
The QC service shall initiate quality checking processes immediately upon detection of newly available data, without manual intervention.
The QC service shall apply the appropriate set of quality checks based on the data type and source of the newly available data.
The QC service shall complete the quality checking process within a configurable maximum time interval after data become available.
The QC service shall log the timestamp of data arrival and the timestamp of quality check initiation to support monitoring of processing latency.
The QC service shall queue incoming data for quality checking in the order of arrival when multiple data sets become available simultaneously.
The QC service shall generate an alert if quality checking cannot be initiated within the required time after data become available.
The DM service shall notify the QC service immediately when new data are ingested and made available in the data store.
The DM service shall provide access to newly available data in a format compatible with the quality checking process requirements.
The QC service shall support concurrent quality checking of multiple independent data sets to minimize processing delays.
The QC service shall record the results of each quality check, including pass/fail status and any identified anomalies, immediately upon completion.
The QC service shall make quality-checked data available for downstream processing immediately after the quality checking process is completed.
